meaning: Continue at the specified altitude, heading, or speed until further advised.
simpleMeaning: Keep doing what ATC told you — same altitude, heading, or speed.
portugueseMeaning: Manter altitude, proa ou velocidade conforme instruído.
operationalMeaning: Maintain is a restriction — you stay at the assigned value until ATC changes it.
whoUsesIt: All ATC positions; pilots read back and comply.
whenUsed: Climb/descent clearances, speed control on approach, runway heading after departure.
whyUsed: Creates predictable flight paths for separation and sequencing.

realExamples.text: Climb and maintain five thousand feet.
realExamples.speaker: Departure

realExamples.text: Maintain runway heading until passing three thousand feet.
realExamples.speaker: Tower

realExamples.text: Maintain two five zero knots until five mile final.
realExamples.speaker: Approach

pilotResponses: Climb and maintain five thousand feet, ANAC 123.
Maintaining two five zero knots, ANAC 123.
icaoQuestions: What is the difference between 'climb to' and 'climb and maintain'?
When would Tower tell you to maintain runway heading?
commonMistakes: Stopping a climb at 'to' altitude when clearance said 'climb and maintain'.
Leaving an assigned speed early on final.
Translating as 'manter' on frequency — use maintain in English.
pronunciationTips: Altitude: flight level FOUR ZERO, not forty thousand.

compareTerms.note: Descend changes altitude downward; maintain holds a value.
Expect prepares you for a future clearance; maintain is current.

didYouKnow: Maintain speed assignments are common on busy approaches into São Paulo.
captainStory: After takeoff from Congonhas, Tower said maintain runway heading — the FO kept the departure path clean until Departure picked us up on radar.
